destinations.evidence: Port scheduler helper parses SCHEDULER_ADDRESS when set and otherwise uses the current fallback address.
Nodebuilder add-ons parse SCHEDULER_ADDRESS and use the same fallback address when unset.

equivalence_argument: Mapped locally: the port Scheduler helper and nodebuilder add-ons use the same fallback and support SCHEDULER_ADDRESS override.
rationale: The assigned and neighbor candidates establish the invariant family: scheduler address selection is environment-overridable and fallback-address updates must stay consistent across add-on and binary initialization.
statement: Both scheduler initialization paths should parse a valid SCHEDULER_ADDRESS when set; when unset, they should use the same hard-coded scheduler address. Current local evidence shows both paths using 0x56e776BAE2DD60664b69Bd5F865F1180ffB7D58B as the fallback.
suggested_falsification_tests: Unset SCHEDULER_ADDRESS and assert both paths return 0x56e776BAE2DD60664b69Bd5F865F1180ffB7D58B.
Set SCHEDULER_ADDRESS to a valid alternate and assert both parse/use it.
summary: Scheduler address fallback and override are aligned locally.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Scheduler address override/fallback unit tests in port helper and nodebuilder add-ons.
title: Scheduler address defaults stay aligned and overridable
